kicker: ET LEVEL I // FROM ARAGO TO ARRAYS

beats.narration: Welcome to eddy current testing: a coil reads a conductive part without ever touching it, sensing flaws through the change it feels.

payload.title: EDDY CURRENT TESTING
payload.sub: Reading the part without touching it.

beats.narration: Eighteen twenty-four: Arago's spinning copper disk drags a compass needle. Eighteen thirty-one: Faraday explains induction. Eighteen thirty-four: Lenz adds the direction.
Drive a coil with alternating current. Its changing field induces swirling eddy currents in the part. Those currents push back and shift the coil's impedance.

payload.heading: How an eddy current probe works
payload.note: COIL FIELD → EDDY CURRENTS → OPPOSING FIELD → IMPEDANCE SHIFT

beats.narration: ET's lane: conductive metals only, strongest at the surface. No couplant, no consumables — it even inspects through paint. Cracks, conductivity, coating thickness.
Two centuries on, every probe still replays it: drive the coil, induce the currents, read the pushback.

payload.sub: Two centuries of physics in every probe.
